content:
# Romans Chapter 6 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/ROM_006_page_001.jpg

1. What then shall we say? Shall we remain in the sin that the grace may increase?

2. May it never be! We who died to the sin, how shall we still live in it?

3. Or are you ignorant that as many [of us] as were baptized into Christos Yeshua, into the death of him we were baptized?

4. Therefore we were buried together with him through the baptism into the death, that just as Christos was raised from [the] dead through the glory of the Father, so also we might walk in newness of life.

5. For if we have become united in the likeness of the death of him, certainly we will also be [united in the likeness] of the resurrection,

6. knowing this, that the old human of us was crucified together [with him], that the body of the sin might be rendered powerless, so that we no longer serve the sin.

7. For the [one] having died has been justified from the sin.

8. And if we died with Christos, we believe that we will also live with him,

9. knowing that Christos, having been raised from [the] dead, no longer dies; death no longer has lordship over him.

10. For [the death] which he died, to the sin he died once for all; but [the life] which he lives, he lives to the God.

11. So also you, reckon yourselves to be dead indeed to the sin, but living to the God in Christos Yeshua.

12. Therefore do not let the sin reign in the mortal body of you, to obey the desires of it.

13. Neither present the members of you as weapons of unrighteousness to the sin, but present yourselves to the God as [those] living from [the] dead, and the members of you as weapons of righteousness to the God.

14. For sin will not have lordship over you; for you are not under law but under grace.

15. What then? Shall we sin because we are not under law but under grace? May it never be!

16. Do you not know that to whom you present yourselves as slaves for obedience, you are slaves of the [one] whom you obey, whether of sin to death, or of obedience to righteousness?

17. But thanks to the God that you were slaves of the sin, but you obeyed from [the] heart the form of teaching to which you were handed over,

18. and having been freed from the sin, you were enslaved to the righteousness.

19. I speak in human [terms] because of the weakness of the flesh of you. For just as you presented the members of you as slaves to the uncleanness and to the lawlessness for lawlessness, so now present the members of you as slaves to the righteousness for holiness.

20. For when you were slaves of the sin, you were free in regard to the righteousness.

21. What fruit therefore were you having then? [Things] of which you are now ashamed; for the end of those things [is] death.

22. But now, having been freed from the sin and having been enslaved to the God, you have the fruit of you for holiness, and the end — eternal life.

23. For the wages of the sin [is] death, but the gift of the God [is] eternal life in Christos Yeshua the Lord of us.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.3 — "Baptized into his death":** εἰς τὸν θάνατον αὐτοῦ ἐβαπτίσθημεν. Paul's baptismal theology is participatory, not merely symbolic. Baptism is participation in Christ's death — a real union, not mere illustration.

2. **v.6 — "The old human" (ὁ παλαιὸς ἡμῶν ἄνθρωπος):** Literally "the old human of us." This is the Adamic self, the person shaped by the regime of sin and death. "Crucified together" (συνεσταυρώθη) — the old self dies the same death Christ died.

3. **v.13 — "Weapons" (ὅπλα):** Can also mean "instruments" or "tools." The military metaphor is apt: members of the body are either weaponized for sin or weaponized for righteousness. There is no neutral position.

4. **v.23 — "Wages" vs. "gift":** ὀψώνια (wages — a soldier's pay) versus χάρισμα (free gift). The contrast is deliberate: sin pays a wage you earn (death); God gives a gift you don't earn (eternal life). The economics of sin and grace are opposite.

— EMET, AI Translator
